  5. Best Wedding Venues in Maryland for 2026: Complete Guide
Venue Guides 18 min read

Best Wedding Venues in Maryland for 2026: Complete Guide

25+ Maryland wedding venues by region: Baltimore, Annapolis, Eastern Shore, DC suburbs. Real pricing and capacity details from a Maryland wedding planner.

J

John

Baltimore Wedding Planner

Featured image, replace with real photo

Maryland has more variety in wedding venues than most couples realize. You've got waterfront estates on the Chesapeake Bay, historic ballrooms in downtown Baltimore, arboretums in the city's parks, converted industrial spaces in the arts district, barns and farm estates in the western counties, and Annapolis manor houses that date back to the 1700s.

As a Baltimore-based wedding planner, I've worked events at venues across the entire state. I know which ones deliver on their photos, which ones have catering restrictions that'll blow your budget, and which ones are genuinely good value for what you get. This guide covers 25+ venues by region with honest pricing and the details venues don't put on their websites.

What Are the Best Wedding Venues in Baltimore, Maryland?

Baltimore's wedding venue scene is strong for mid-range and budget couples. The city's industrial history left behind beautiful warehouse and mill spaces, and the historic neighborhoods are full of mansion-style venues. Here are the ones worth seriously considering.

Evergreen Museum and Library

Historic Up to 200 guests $5,000 to $10,000 venue fee

A 48-room Tudor Revival mansion in North Baltimore with formal gardens and one of the most beautiful ceremony backdrops in the city. The mansion's a museum, which means you're getting married in a preserved historic estate with art on every wall. Venue fee typically runs $5,000 to $10,000 and includes outdoor ceremony space, indoor reception rooms, and use of the gardens. Catering's through approved vendors. Book 12 to 18 months out for peak season dates.

Clipper Mill

Industrial Up to 250 guests $3,000 to $7,000 venue fee

A converted 19th-century iron foundry in Hampden with exposed brick, original timber beams, and cobblestone courtyards. It's one of Baltimore's most photographed industrial wedding spaces. The venue allows outside catering through a preferred vendor list and has a genuine industrial-chic aesthetic that photographs exceptionally well. Weekend rates typically run $3,000 to $7,000 depending on the space configuration and season.

Motor House

Arts / Industrial Up to 150 guests $1,500 to $3,500 venue fee

Located in Station North, Baltimore's arts and entertainment district, Motor House is one of the city's best budget-friendly options for couples who want an industrial vibe without the premium price. The raw space works well for creative weddings. Outside catering is allowed. The main hall holds up to 150 comfortably. Venue fees are among the most accessible in Baltimore at $1,500 to $3,500, making it a strong choice for couples working with a $10,000 to $15,000 total budget.

The Engineers Club

Historic Mansion Up to 175 guests $4,000 to $8,000 venue fee

A Beaux-Arts mansion in Mount Vernon, Baltimore's historic cultural district. The ballroom has original plasterwork ceilings, chandeliers, and the kind of architectural detail you can't fake. In-house catering is available through their culinary team. The venue seats up to 175 for dinner and has a separate cocktail hour space. Venue fees run $4,000 to $8,000 depending on the day and season. Saturday evenings in peak season are on the higher end.

Cylburn Arboretum

Garden / Outdoor Up to 200 guests $1,500 to $4,000 venue fee

A 207-acre arboretum in North Baltimore City with formal gardens, Victorian mansion grounds, and mature tree canopies. It's one of the best outdoor venue values in the city. The arboretum's a nonprofit, which means venue fees are significantly lower than comparable private estates. You'll need to bring in outside catering and a tent rental for the reception. Best for couples who want natural beauty and don't mind coordinating rentals. Fees run $1,500 to $4,000.

Patterson Park

Outdoor / Park Up to 100 guests (permit-free under 25) $50 to $300 permit fee

One of Baltimore's largest public parks, famous for its historic pagoda overlooking the park grounds. Small ceremonies under 25 people don't require a permit. Larger groups need a Baltimore City parks permit ($50 to $300 depending on size). The pagoda backdrop is one of the most distinct and recognized ceremony settings in Baltimore. You'll need to rent everything (chairs, arch, sound system) and arrange outside catering. Total ceremony costs can stay under $2,000 for a small group with smart vendor selection.

What Are the Best Wedding Venues in Annapolis, Maryland?

Annapolis venues command a premium for the setting. Chesapeake Bay waterfront access, colonial-era architecture, and year-round demand from Naval Academy families and DC-area couples mean prices run 15 to 25% higher than comparable Baltimore spaces. If Annapolis is what you're envisioning, here's where to look.

Quiet Waters Park

Waterfront / Park Up to 300 guests $500 to $2,000 permit/rental fee

A 340-acre Anne Arundel County park on the South River with waterfront pavilions, formal gardens, and wooded ceremony sites. The David A. Winston Garden is the most popular ceremony spot, with river views and manicured grounds. Indoor pavilions accommodate up to 300 for reception. Park pavilion rental runs $500 to $2,000 depending on the space and day. Outside catering is allowed through the park's approved vendor list. One of the best value waterfront venues in the Annapolis area and one I recommend frequently for elopements and micro-weddings.

Annapolis Maritime Museum

Waterfront Up to 150 guests $3,000 to $6,000 venue fee

Located on Back Creek in Eastport, with direct Chesapeake Bay views and a working waterfront setting. Ceremony and reception space is available both indoors and on the waterfront lawn. Venue fees run $3,000 to $6,000. Outside catering is permitted through approved vendors. The maritime setting photographs beautifully, especially for late afternoon and early evening ceremonies. Capacity is 150, which makes it best for mid-size weddings.

William Paca House and Garden

Historic / Garden Up to 100 guests $2,500 to $5,000 venue fee

A preserved 18th-century colonial mansion with a five-part garden that's one of the most authentically historic ceremony settings in Maryland. The gardens are tiered and formal, with boxwood hedges, a fish pond, and original colonial plantings. Ceremony capacity is limited to around 100, making it best for intimate weddings. Venue fees run $2,500 to $5,000. Outside catering permitted through approved vendors. If your vision is colonial Maryland history, there's nothing else in the state quite like this.

Historic Inns of Annapolis

Historic Hotel Up to 200 guests $5,000 to $12,000 venue fee

A collection of three 18th-century buildings in historic downtown Annapolis, steps from the State House and City Dock. The Governor Calvert House ballroom is the primary event space and seats up to 200 for dinner. In-house catering is available. The location is genuinely historic and puts your guests in the heart of downtown Annapolis. Venue fees run $5,000 to $12,000 depending on the event space and season. On the higher end for the Annapolis market, but the location and historic architecture justify it for couples who prioritize setting.

What Are the Best Wedding Venues on Maryland's Eastern Shore?

Eastern Shore venues deliver something different: Chesapeake Bay waterfront access without the Annapolis prices, plus a slower pace and rural setting that suits couples who want their wedding to feel like a getaway. The drive from Baltimore is about 90 minutes. From DC, about 2 hours.

Chesapeake Bay Maritime Museum (St. Michaels)

Waterfront Up to 300 guests $4,000 to $8,000 venue fee

Located directly on the Miles River in St. Michaels, one of the most charming towns on Maryland's Eastern Shore. The museum campus includes waterfront outdoor ceremony space and indoor reception areas. You're surrounded by skipjacks, historic boats, and the bay. Outside catering is permitted. Fees run $4,000 to $8,000. If you want Chesapeake Bay authenticity rather than just water views, this is one of the most distinctive venues in Maryland.

Tilghman Island Inn

Waterfront Inn Up to 100 guests Inquire for pricing

A waterfront inn on Tilghman Island with Chesapeake Bay views from virtually every spot on the property. Small-to-mid-size weddings and elopements are what this venue does best. The setting is genuinely stunning at sunset. Guests can stay on the property, which simplifies logistics considerably. Best for intimate weddings under 100 guests who want a destination feel without a destination budget.

Eastern Shore Farm and Barn Venues

Barn / Farm Up to 250 guests $2,500 to $6,000 venue fee

The Eastern Shore has a genuine agricultural landscape that works beautifully for barn and farm weddings. Several private farms in Queen Anne's, Talbot, and Dorchester counties rent their properties for weddings. Pricing is typically $2,500 to $6,000 for full-day use. You'll coordinate all rentals (tent, chairs, tables, sound) and bring in outside catering, but the setting and flexibility are hard to beat for couples who want a countryside Maryland feel.

What Are the Best Outdoor Wedding Venues in Maryland?

Maryland's outdoor venue scene runs the full range from state parks and arboretums to private estates and mountain retreats. The key variable is how much infrastructure you'll need to bring in versus what's already included.

Cunningham Falls State Park (Frederick County)

Mountain / Waterfall Small groups $50 to $200 permit fee

Maryland's largest waterfall is in Catoctin Mountain Park near Thurmont. Ceremony permits through the Maryland Department of Natural Resources run $50 to $200. The waterfall setting is genuinely dramatic and works beautifully for elopements and very small ceremonies under 30 people. Getting guests to the falls requires a short hike, which limits this to mobile guests but creates an unforgettable ceremony location. Recommended for elopements and adventurous micro-weddings.

Assateague Island National Seashore

Beach / National Park Small groups $150 National Park permit

A barrier island on Maryland's Atlantic coast, best known for its wild ponies. The beach ceremony setting is Atlantic-facing, with wide open sky and the kind of photos that don't look like they were taken in Maryland. National Park Service permit runs $150. Ceremonies must be kept small (under 25 for most permits). Best for couples planning a genuine beach elopement rather than a traditional wedding.

Oregon Ridge Park (Baltimore County)

Woodland / Lake Up to 150 guests $500 to $1,500 permit fee

A Baltimore County park with a small lake, wooded hillsides, and a nature center that includes indoor reception space. One of the most accessible outdoor venue options for Baltimore-area couples who want nature without traveling far. Permits run $500 to $1,500. Outside catering is allowed. The lodge can accommodate up to 150 indoors, with outdoor ceremony areas along the lake. Genuinely underused relative to how good it is.

What Are the Best Maryland Wedding Venues for Small Weddings Under 50 Guests?

Small weddings in Maryland aren't just cheaper versions of big ones. They're a different kind of event. The venue options for under 50 guests open up significantly, and the per-person cost often goes down.

Best small venue options by type: private estate rentals in Baltimore County ($1,500 to $3,500), historic house museums with small-event minimums (Homewood Museum, Hampton National Historic Site), restaurant private dining rooms in Baltimore and Annapolis ($0 to $1,500 room rental, food minimum applies), Annapolis waterfront parks for ceremonies with a catered dinner elsewhere, and Maryland state park pavilions in the $200 to $800 range for outdoor ceremonies with picnic-style receptions.

How Do Maryland Wedding Venue Costs Compare by Region?

Venue rental is typically 25 to 35% of a Maryland wedding budget. Here's how costs break down across regions for a comparable venue quality level.

Region Budget Venue Mid-Range Venue Premium Venue
Baltimore City$1,500 to $3,500$3,500 to $7,000$7,000 to $15,000+
Annapolis$2,000 to $4,500$4,500 to $9,000$9,000 to $20,000+
Eastern Shore$1,500 to $3,000$3,000 to $6,000$6,000 to $12,000
Western Maryland$1,000 to $2,500$2,500 to $5,000$5,000 to $10,000
DC Suburbs (PG/Montgomery)$2,500 to $5,000$5,000 to $10,000$10,000 to $25,000+
State / County Parks$50 to $500$500 to $2,000N/A

How Do You Choose the Right Maryland Wedding Venue?

The venue decision is where most Maryland wedding budgets succeed or fail. Here are the five questions that matter most before you sign a contract.

  1. What's the catering policy? All-inclusive venues (in-house catering only) can add $50 to $100 per person in mandatory minimums. Open catering venues let you hire your own caterer and potentially save $2,000 to $6,000. Always ask before touring.
  2. What's not included in the venue fee? Tables, chairs, linens, sound system, lighting, parking attendants, and cleanup fees often aren't included. A $3,000 venue fee can become $5,000 after mandatory add-ons.
  3. What's the weather contingency? For outdoor Maryland venues, ask whether there's an indoor backup if it rains. If not, you're renting a tent ($1,500 to $4,000) as insurance.
  4. What are the noise and time restrictions? Many Maryland venues, especially in residential areas and parks, require music off by 10pm or 11pm. If you're planning a late reception, confirm the cutoff.
  5. How far out are dates available? Popular Maryland venues book 12 to 18 months in advance for peak season Saturdays. If your preferred date is less than 6 months away, expect limited availability in Baltimore and Annapolis.

Working with a Maryland wedding planner helps here.

John has relationships with venues across the state and knows which ones have hidden costs, which ones are genuinely easy to work with, and which ones are worth the premium. He can narrow your venue search to the right two or three options for your budget and guest count.

See Full-Service Planning

Frequently Asked Questions About Maryland Wedding Venues

What's the most affordable wedding venue in Baltimore?

For outdoor ceremonies, Patterson Park requires no permit for groups under 25 and costs just $50 to $300 for larger groups. For indoor receptions, Motor House in Station North runs $1,500 to $3,500 and allows outside catering, making it one of the most budget-friendly options with genuine character. Cylburn Arboretum is another strong choice at $1,500 to $4,000 for a garden setting with much more space than most city venues at that price point.

Which Maryland wedding venues include catering?

Venues with in-house catering (or exclusive caterer requirements) include the Historic Inns of Annapolis, The Engineers Club in Baltimore, and most hotel ballrooms (Westin Annapolis, Renaissance Baltimore, etc.). These venues simplify vendor coordination but typically add $70 to $150 per person in catering minimums. Venues that allow outside catering include Cylburn Arboretum, Oregon Ridge Park, Clipper Mill, and most state and county parks.

How far in advance do you need to book a Maryland wedding venue?

For peak season Saturday dates (May through October) at popular Baltimore and Annapolis venues, 12 to 18 months in advance is standard. For Friday or Sunday dates, you can sometimes book 6 to 9 months out. Off-season dates (November through April) have much better availability and can sometimes be secured in 3 to 6 months. State park permits are available on a first-come basis and can be booked 6 months to a year in advance through the Maryland DNR or county parks systems.

Are there good Maryland wedding venues for under $3,000?

Yes. The best options under $3,000 in venue rental fees are: state and county park pavilions ($200 to $2,000), Motor House in Baltimore ($1,500 to $3,500), Cylburn Arboretum gardens ($1,500 to $4,000 with flexibility on the lower end for weekdays), Eastern Shore farm rentals (some as low as $1,500 for weekday events), and Patterson Park for small outdoor ceremonies (under $300 for the permit). Many of these require you to bring in outside catering and rentals, so factor those costs into your comparison.

Which Maryland wedding venues are best for outdoor ceremonies with an indoor reception backup?

Venues that offer both outdoor ceremony space and indoor reception space with a built-in weather backup include Quiet Waters Park (waterfront pavilion), Oregon Ridge Park (nature center lodge), Evergreen Museum and Library (mansion plus gardens), and The Engineers Club (courtyard plus indoor ballroom). If you're booking a primarily outdoor Maryland venue without indoor backup, budget $1,500 to $4,000 for a tent rental as weather insurance. Maryland spring and fall weddings can have unpredictable weather.

John has coordinated weddings at venues across Maryland. If you're considering a venue not on this list, he's likely familiar with it.
